District & Region Updates.title: DM message: peak prep checklist
District & Region Updates.teaser: Four peak-prep items to lock in over the next two weeks.
District & Region Updates.content: Stores in District 12 — four items: confirm peak schedule 3 weeks out; clear backroom; pre-stage hot SKUs; confirm fitting-room and cash-wrap coverage.

District & Region Updates.title: Regional contest results — South Region
District & Region Updates.teaser: Top three stores get a team-celebration budget.
District & Region Updates.content: South Region customer-service contest results. Top three: Orlando D-6 (0779), Atlanta Metro D-3 (0617), Central Florida D-7 (0512).

Store News.title: Tampa flagship lobby refresh
Store News.teaser: New signage, repainted walls, brighter entry.
Store News.content: The Tampa flagship lobby refresh wrapped this week. The refresh program continues — 100+ stores this fiscal year.

Store News.title: Grand opening recap — new Brooklyn store
Store News.teaser: Line around the block. Record opening-day volume.
Store News.content: The new Brooklyn store opened last weekend with a line around the block. Record volume for an opening day.

Store News.title: Customer-service callout — Sacramento crew
Store News.teaser: Best CS scores in the NorCal district this month.
Store News.content: Sacramento posted the best CS scores in NorCal this month. The DM: “Their team is consistent, friendly, and fast on the floor.”

Store News.title: BOPIS pilot — where we're testing it
Store News.content: A small BOPIS pilot is running in a handful of stores across two districts. We'll share what we learn by end of quarter.

IT Security.title: Phishing of the month — fake schedule alert
IT Security.teaser: Three red flags. Forward to itsecurity@ross.com.
IT Security.content: This month's phishing example: an email pretending to be a schedule alert. Red flags: non-Ross sender, non-Ross URL, urgency. Forward to itsecurity@ross.com and delete.

Quick Takes.title: 60-sec town-hall recap — peak prep checklist
Quick Takes.teaser: Peak prep, app updates, training modules.
Quick Takes.content: Quick recap: receiving cadence locked in; scheduling-app updates next week; new training modules live on Ross Connect.

Quick Takes.title: Meet a buyer — 30 seconds with Priya
Quick Takes.teaser: Priya is a Ladies' Apparel buyer in NYC.
Quick Takes.content: Priya is one of our Ladies' Apparel buyers in NYC. Her one-liner: “Every Monday is a new puzzle.”

Quick Takes.title: Store walk — Tampa flagship
Quick Takes.teaser: Clean floor, sharp signage, fast fitting-room turn.
Quick Takes.content: Quick walk through Tampa flagship today. Thanks team.

Quick Takes.title: Receiving tip of the day
Quick Takes.teaser: Stage carts by department before pulling tags.
Quick Takes.content: If you're processing freight: stage carts by department before pulling tags. Cuts walk time roughly in half.

Quick Takes.title: New SOP heads-up
Quick Takes.content: New backroom-organization SOP drops Monday. Two-pager, big diagrams. Ask your DM by Tuesday.

Ross on LinkedIn.title: Ross featured in industry coverage this week
Ross on LinkedIn.teaser: Trade press on off-price's continued share gains.
Ross on LinkedIn.content: Ross was featured this week in industry trade press covering off-price's continued share gains. A great moment to be proud of where we work.

Top News.title: Jim Conroy named CEO of Ross Stores
Top News.teaser: James G. Conroy, former Boot Barn CEO, took over as CEO Feb 2, 2025. Barbara Rentler moved to an advisory role through March 2027.
Top News.content:
Ross Stores' Board appointed James “Jim” Conroy — former CEO of Boot Barn — as Chief Executive Officer, effective February 2, 2025. Jim joined Ross on December 2, 2024 as CEO-Elect.

Barbara Rentler, CEO for over a decade, transitioned to an advisory role through March 31, 2027.

Executive Chairman Michael Balmuth: “We are confident that Jim's track record of leveraging customer-centric retail strategies to maximize top- and bottom-line results will help drive our Company's profitable growth and shareholder value.”

Thank you to Barbara — and welcome, Jim.

Source: Ross Stores Investor Relations.

Top News.title: Ross Stores reports record Q4 — sales up 12%, comps up 9%
Top News.teaser: Q4 came in well above guidance. CEO Jim Conroy: business momentum accelerated through the quarter. Board approves 10% dividend increase.
Top News.content:
Ross Stores reported Q4 fiscal 2025 results well above expectations: sales up 12% and comparable store sales up 9%, with EPS of $2.00 vs. guidance of $1.77–$1.85. Full-year sales: a record $22.8 billion.

Jim Conroy: “Business momentum accelerated further in the fourth quarter, with both sales and earnings significantly surpassing expectations.”

The Board approved a new two-year $2.55 billion share repurchase authorization and a 10% dividend increase to $0.445 per share.

Thank you to every associate across stores, DCs, buying offices, and Home Office for the work behind these results.

Source: Ross Stores Investor Relations.

Top News.title: Sustainability milestone — 31% GHG reduction per sq. ft. since 2017
Top News.teaser: Ross exceeded its near-term GHG target. Next milestone: 42% absolute by 2030; net-zero by 2050.
Top News.content:
Ross's latest CSR report shows a 31% reduction in Scope 1 + 2 GHG emissions per square foot vs. our 2017 baseline — exceeding our near-term 30%-by-2025 target.

  • Science-aligned interim goal: 42% absolute Scope 1 + 2 reduction by 2030.
  • Long-term ambition: net-zero by 2050 or sooner.
  • Waste: 66% diversion from landfill.

Ross also earned a “B” score on the CDP 2024 Climate Change disclosure.

Source: corp.rossstores.com.

Crisis Hub News.title: Hurricane prep — Southeast stores and DCs
Crisis Hub News.content: Standard hurricane prep: 72hr — secure signage, prep supplies; 24hr — follow DM closure guidance; after — wait for the all-clear before re-entering.

Crisis Hub News.title: All-clear procedure after a weather event
Crisis Hub News.teaser: Who issues it, who walks first.
Crisis Hub News.content: An all-clear must be issued by the DM or RVP before any associate re-enters a store or DC. First walkthrough is by the manager + LP partner.

Crisis Hub News.title: Store closure procedure — emergency closes
Crisis Hub News.teaser: Five steps + where to update the Crisis Hub.
Crisis Hub News.content: Emergency-close procedure: 1) notify DM; 2) stop sales + lock cash room; 3) post closure notice; 4) account for every associate; 5) update Crisis Hub.

Urgent Updates.title: New time-off system rolls out company-wide
Urgent Updates.teaser: New portal goes live the first of next month.
Urgent Updates.content: The new time-off and PTO request system goes live company-wide on the first. Existing requests will migrate automatically. Training videos on Ross Connect.

From Jim's Desk.title: Hello from Jim — first impressions, and a thank-you to the team
From Jim's Desk.teaser: A note from Jim Conroy after his first weeks on the road visiting Ross stores, DCs, and the buying offices.
From Jim's Desk.content:
Team — I'm Jim Conroy, and I started as CEO of Ross Stores on February 2. A quick thank-you for the welcome I've gotten on every store and DC visit.

What I'm hearing

  • You know the customer better than anyone. The instincts on what sells and how fast — that's the Ross edge.
  • Receiving cadence is everything. Clean freight flow keeps the floor fresh.
  • The team takes care of each other. Across every store I visited.

To Barbara Rentler — thank you for years of leadership. I'm grateful to step into a company in such great shape.

— Jim

From Jim's Desk.title: A note from the DC floor — the off-price flow advantage
From Jim's Desk.teaser: Jim spent the week at Carlisle, PA. Why DC throughput is the off-price advantage no algorithm can replicate.
From Jim's Desk.content:
I spent this week at our Carlisle, PA distribution center. Carlisle just hit a record week of throughput — thank you to that team.

Off-price is a moving business. Our buyers source thousands of vendor positions every week. None of that matters if we can't process, sort, ship, and get product onto the floor fast.

  • Clean yard discipline. Trailer scheduling, door assignments, dock turn — basics done well.
  • Zero safety incidents this quarter. Heavy peak inbound, no compromises.
  • Cross-team comms. Receiving, processing, and shipping talking to each other.

— Jim

From Jim's Desk.title: Heading into our second half — disciplined, customer-first
From Jim's Desk.teaser: Jim's message to associates as we shift into the second half. Take care of each other. Stay close to the customer. Trust the model.
From Jim's Desk.content:
We're past the midpoint of the year. Q1 strong, Q4 above guidance, store-opening pipeline in great shape.

Three things on my mind

1. Take care of each other. Long shifts, peak freight, more customers — it adds up. Watch out for the associate next to you.

2. Stay close to the customer. People are watching every dollar. Greet them, help them, make the visit easy.

3. Trust the model. Off-price works because we buy smart, flow fast, and let the assortment do the work.

— Jim

From Jim's Desk.title: The treasure hunt is what makes us Ross
From Jim's Desk.teaser: Customers walk into a Ross store for a reason — to find something they weren't expecting. Why that experience is the off-price moat.
From Jim's Desk.content:
Customers walk into a Ross Dress for Less store for one reason: they want to find something they weren't expecting. That's the treasure hunt — and it's the part the internet can't replicate.

  • Buyers in NYC and LA putting deals together every week.
  • DCs turning product around fast — sometimes in under a week.
  • Stores creating the moment of discovery — clean floor, sharp signage, fast fitting room.

To every associate at every store, DC, buying office, and Home Office desk: thank you for keeping the treasure hunt alive.

— Jim

Off-Price Retail News.title: The off-price model — a quick refresher
Off-Price Retail News.teaser: Opportunistic buying, fast flow, no promo cadence, treasure hunt.
Off-Price Retail News.content: Off-price is a different beast. Our merchants buy when vendors have inventory positions they need to move. DCs process and ship quickly so newness lands every week. We don't run sales — our everyday price is the deal.

Off-Price Retail News.title: Industry update — back-to-school recap
Off-Price Retail News.teaser: Off-price was a clear share-gainer this back-to-school.
Off-Price Retail News.content: The off-price segment had a strong back-to-school. Industry analysts called out our category as a share-gainer — good sign for the holiday quarter.

Buying Office & DC News.title: Buying team trip recap — markets in NYC
Buying Office & DC News.teaser: Five days, thousands of vendor walks. How NYC sources the next round of treasures.
Buying Office & DC News.content: Our NYC buying team wrapped market week. Off-price buying is a different model — we move fast, take opportunistic positions, chase what's working in real time.

Buying Office & DC News.content: The Perris, CA DC processed a record number of units last week. Clean yard schedule, strong cross-team communication, zero safety incidents.

Buying Office & DC News.content: We're updating Return-to-Vendor in November. Electronic RTV form; fixed weekly DC pickup; new backroom bin tags shipped to stores this month.

Buying Office & DC News.title: Freight planning for peak — what stores will see
Buying Office & DC News.teaser: Heavier truck deliveries soon. Three reminders that keep peak flow clean.
Buying Office & DC News.content: Peak freight volume is ramping. Schedule receiving labor when the truck arrives. Process by department. Communicate DC issues to your DM right away.

Associate Spotlight.title: Asset protection win in our Las Vegas store

Associate Spotlight.content: Big shoutout to Las Vegas for outstanding LP results this quarter — shrink down 30%+ year over year. Loss prevention is every associate noticing, greeting, and recovering.

Associate Spotlight.title: A customer thank-you note that says it all

The associate is Aisha — and we did pass it along.

Health & Safety.title: Slip, trip and fall — the basics every shift
Health & Safety.teaser: Three things every shift to keep our salesfloors safe.
Health & Safety.content: Slips, trips and falls are the most common cause of associate injuries. Pick up tags and hangers as you walk. Wet-floor signs go out before you mop. Move cords/boxes in walkways.

Health & Safety.title: Peak-season safety reminders
Health & Safety.content: Peak means more freight, more customers, more movement. Ladders: never reach. Box cutters: blade away. Lifting: bend at knees; help over 35 lbs. Hydration: drink water on long shifts.

Health & Safety.title: Heat illness — DC associates
Health & Safety.teaser: Recognize heat exhaustion vs. heat stroke.
Health & Safety.content: For DC teams in warmer climates: heat illness is a real risk. Heat exhaustion — heavy sweating, weakness, cool/clammy skin: move to cool area, hydrate, tell supervisor. Heat stroke — hot/dry skin, confusion: call 911.

Health & Safety.title: Ladder safety on the salesfloor
Health & Safety.teaser: Three points of contact, never the top step.
Health & Safety.content: Quick refresher: inspect before each use. Three points of contact. Don't carry merchandise up — pass it up. Never use the top step. Tag out anything wobbly.
